surface_verification.justification: Framework merge-tooling completeness fix: admits .shipwright/compliance/test-traceability.json to the churn allowlist + regenerate-staging + integrate rollback (mirror of the ci-security.json CR-1). No product/source, API, store, SSE/WS or UI code touched, so spec_impact is none and the E2E/cross-layer gates do not fire. Verified end-to-end by real-git merge-conflict integration tests (resolve + rollback) in the Python suite.

behaviors.behavior: conflicted test-traceability.json is resolvable (not blocking) in classify/complete_merge
regenerate records + STAGES test-traceability.json when _update_compliance rewrites it
regenerate does not phantom-stage an unchanged test-traceability.json
a real two-sided merge conflict on test-traceability.json RESOLVES through integrate_main (regenerated content reaches the follow-up commit, clean tree) instead of aborting
integrate_main rollback restores test-traceability.json on a post-regen failure (no dirty residue)
hooks-and-pipeline churn table stays 1:1 with CHURN_ALLOWLIST both directions
